title: Include "host.containers.internal" entry during the image build process
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

When building an image, it would be nice to have access to the `host.containers.internal` IP address. This allows developers to author build files that consume additional binaries / metadata / authorized content from a service that is running locally on the machine.

This is something which is currently supported by Docker and I am trying to move away from this strong dependency on Docker.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. In the `Dockerfile`, try looking up the `host.containers.internal`.

**Describe the results you received:**
The hostname `host.containers.internal` does not resolve when building the image.

**Describe the results you expected:**
The hostname `host.containers.internal` should be resolvable during the image-building process and should point to the host running podman

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.17
Built:        Mon Aug 30 12:15:26 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.3.0
API Version:  3.3.0
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Fri Aug 20 12:36:14 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
@roguexz interested in opening a PR?
@flouthoc PTAL

@rhatdan I would love to do that .. just that I am Java guy who can make Jackson de-serialize data into an interface! ... but when it comes to Go, I will need an example to start with. If you can point me in the right direction, I can give it a shot.

Simplest route would be to look for /etc/hosts within  the buildah code base.  Then look at the code that does this in podman

./libpod/container_internal_linux.go:				hosts += fmt.Sprintf("%s host.containers.internal\n", ip.Gateway)

Then figure out how to copy it accross.
If you know java you can figure out golang.  Especially if you are only making a small change.  Writing the tests might be a little difficult, but you can probably steel something from Podman.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
